fractions_decimals.questionBridge: Simon dips his pen and slides the ledger toward you. One entry refuses to balance.

ratios_proportions.settingLine: Giza, foot of the pyramid, 600 BC
ratios_proportions.questionBridge: Thales plants his staff in the sand and measures its shadow. Now he turns to you.

order_of_operations.questionBridge: Ada taps the stack of punch cards. One step out of order and the machine fails.

basic_equations.questionBridge: Diophantus loved hiding numbers inside riddles. He scratches a fresh one into the wax.

linear_equations.questionBridge: William checks his watch against the noon sighting. The navigator hands you the numbers.

functions_basics.questionBridge: Leonhard scrawls f(x) on fresh paper, a brand-new shorthand. He tests it on you first.

right_triangle_geometry.questionBridge: The flood retreats. Ahmes stretches the knotted rope taut across the mud and waves you closer.

trigonometry_basics.questionBridge: Hipparchus sights a star and lowers his instrument. He needs this angle checked tonight.

linear_inequalities.questionBridge: William watches another cargo plane touch down. Every flight has limits, and lives ride on them.

systems_of_linear_equations.questionBridge: Liu Hui lays counting rods in careful rows and columns, then looks up at you.

exponent_rules.questionBridge: Jacob compounds the interest again and again. The pattern in the powers stops him cold.

polynomials.questionBridge: Omar sets down his cup and sketches a curve in the margin of his manuscript.

factoring_polynomials.questionBridge: François, the king's codebreaker, sees every equation as a cipher. He hands you one to crack.

radical_expressions.questionBridge: Hippasus lowers his voice on the deck. Some numbers, he whispers, cannot be written down.

quadratic_equations.questionBridge: Muhammad sketches a square in the courtyard dust and begins completing it, piece by piece.

descriptive_statistics.questionBridge: Florence walks the ward at midnight, counting. The numbers must be made to speak.

basic_probability.questionBridge: Cardano sweeps the coins aside and leans across the tavern table with a wager.
basic_probability.diceFrame: Cardano shakes the dice in his cupped hands and slams them onto the table.
basic_probability.spinnerFrame: Cardano flicks the pointer into a blur and dares you to call where it stops.

exponential_functions.questionBridge: John has spent twenty years on his tables. He shows you how fast doubling runs away.

logarithmic_functions.questionBridge: John slides his great table across the desk. Multiplication, he says, can become addition.

sequences_series.questionBridge: The schoolmaster sets his trap. Carl, age ten, is already reaching for his slate.

lines_angles.questionBridge: Euclid draws two lines crossing in the sand and waits for you to see it.

triangles_congruence.questionBridge: Euclid lays one triangle over another. Prove they match, he says, and everything follows.

circles_geometry.questionBridge: Archimedes drags his stick through the sand, tracing a perfect circle around your feet.

area_volume.questionBridge: Archimedes leaps from the bath, still dripping. He needs you to measure what he found.

geometric_transformations.questionBridge: Maurits stares at the Alhambra tiles for hours. Every shape slides, flips, and repeats forever.

rational_expressions.questionBridge: Gottfried reads Newton's latest letter and sharpens his quill. Every ratio must be airtight.

complex_numbers.questionBridge: Rafael refuses to throw the impossible number away. He follows it, and an answer appears.

vectors.questionBridge: James watches the compass needle swing and reaches for arrows to describe the invisible.

matrices.questionBridge: Arthur arranges the numbers into a neat grid, then discovers the grids themselves obey algebra.

conic_sections.questionBridge: Johannes checks Tycho's data one more time. The orbit of Mars refuses to be a circle.

probability_distributions.questionBridge: Abraham names the odds for gamblers over cold coffee. A curve is hiding inside the chaos.
probability_distributions.diceFrame: Abraham stacks the tally of a thousand dice throws and points at the shape emerging.

applications_of_derivatives.questionBridge: Isaac watches the apple fall and wonders how fast, exactly, at each single instant.

integrals.questionBridge: Archimedes slices the shape into slivers thinner than hairs. Now add them all, he says.

applications_of_integrals.questionBridge: John checks the cable calculations one more time. Thousands will cross, so the sums must hold.

inferential_statistics.questionBridge: Ronald walks the muddy field plots at dawn. One harvest proves nothing, unless you test it right.

number_properties.questionBridge: Srinivasa fills another notebook page after his shift. The numbers, he says, confide in him.

measurement_units.questionBridge: Antoine weighs everything twice, even now. The world needs one measure it can trust.

algebraic_manipulation.questionBridge: Muhammad calls it al-jabr, the restoring. Move the piece, he says, but keep the balance.

representation_translation.questionBridge: René watches a fly walk the ceiling and realizes every point can become two numbers.

act_strategy.settingLine: A kitchen table, Saturday morning, present day
act_strategy.questionBridge: Maya flips her practice test over as the timer starts. Every second is a choice.
